hypothesis.original_question: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by at least 30% compared to untreated controls, measured by plasma FITC-dextran levels after oral gavage.

refined.intervention: Daily oral gavage of viable Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus GG (ATCC 53103) at 1×10^9 CFU/mouse/day in 200 µL sterile PBS, for 28 consecutive days
refined.outcome: Plasma FITC-dextran 4 kDa (FD-4) concentration measured 4 h after a single 600 mg/kg oral gavage of FD-4 in sterile PBS, by fluorescence (485/520 nm) against a same-day standard curve in pooled naïve mouse plasma
refined.threshold: Mean plasma FD-4 in LGG arm ≤ 70% of vehicle-control mean (≥ 30% reduction); two-sided Mann–Whitney U test, α = 0.05, design powered for d ≥ 1.0
refined.mechanism: Up-regulation of apical tight-junction proteins (ZO-1, occludin, claudin-1/3/4) and down-regulation of pore-forming claudin-2; soluble factor p40 (Msp1) activates EGFR-PI3K/Akt; competitive exclusion of pathobionts; SCFA-mediated colonocyte support
refined.control: Vehicle-only C57BL/6J mice receiving equal volume of sterile PBS by daily gavage; secondary heat-killed-LGG arm to dissect viability dependence

novelty.rationale: Extensive published LGG → intestinal-barrier literature exists, but ~85% of evidence is from challenge models (DSS colitis, alcohol, HFD, NEC, post-weaning stress), not unstressed adult C57BL/6. The closest published precedent (Singh 2014) was retracted in 2024. The unstressed-baseline question is therefore under-explored *and* the closest match is unreliable. Verdict is 'similar-work-exists' but Pass 3 explicitly flags the precedent quality.

references.title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG Stimulates Dietary Tryptophan-Dependent Production of Barrier-Protecting Methylnicotinamide
references.authors: Suntornsaratoon P, Antonio JM, Flores J, Upadhyay R, Veltri J, Bandyopadhyay S, et al.
references.relevance: Closest in design — uses LGG + FITC-dextran + C57BL/6 mice, including a healthy-mouse arm, with mechanistic depth (tryptophan → MNA → barrier)

references.title: Neonatal Colonization of Mice with LGG Promotes Intestinal Development and Decreases Susceptibility to Colitis in Adult Mice
references.authors: Yan F, Liu L, Cao H, Moore DJ, Washington MK, Wang B, Peek RM, Acra SA, Polk DB
references.relevance: Yan/Polk lineage, in vivo C57BL/6, FD-4 measurement; demonstrates LGG → barrier protection but in a neonatal-imprint design rather than adult unchallenged

references.title: RETRACTED — Improvement in Th1/Th2 immune homeostasis, antioxidative status and resistance to pathogenic E. coli on consumption of probiotic Lactobacillus rhamnosus fermented milk in aging mice
references.relevance: Most direct published precedent — but RETRACTED in 2024. Listed here for transparency: this is the kind of result this hypothesis is testing, and its retraction is itself signal.

protocol.title: Order C57BL/6J mice and acclimate
actions: Order n = 32 C57BL/6J mice (16/arm × 2 arms = 28 + 4 reserve) from JAX, single shipment, single age cohort, 6 weeks at arrival
Single-housed in groups of 4 per cage by treatment arm (8 cages total — 4 LGG, 4 PBS); separate ventilated rack rows for treatment vs control
Standard chow + autoclaved water ad lib
≥ 14 d acclimation before any intervention

reagents.notes: C57BL/6J specifically — pre-registered substrain; do NOT substitute C57BL/6N

failure_warnings.mitigation: Single vendor, single shipment, JAX 000664 documented in methods
Single-vendor sourcing + 14-d acclimation lets local microbiome stabilise before intervention
Treatment and control mice in SEPARATE cages; alternate rack positions; dedicated PPE; control cages handled before LGG cages on every cage-change cycle

protocol.rationale: C57BL/6J pre-registration locks substrain confounder; single shipment + acclimation locks vendor-microbiome confounder; cage design + handling order locks coprophagy.

protocol.title: Establish LGG working stock (single expansion from sealed master)
actions: Order sealed ATCC 53103 vial; thaw and rehydrate per ATCC instructions
Single 10 mL MRS broth starter at 37 °C, static, 16 h to stationary
Expand to 100 mL working culture at 37 °C, harvest at OD₆₀₀ ≈ 1.0
Centrifuge 4 000 ×g 10 min; wash 2× in sterile PBS
Resuspend in 50 % PBS / 50 % sterile glycerol; freeze in 100 single-use 1 mL cryoaliquots at −80 °C
Quality-check: serial-dilute 1 aliquot on MRS-agar (anaerobic, 37 °C, 48 h) → confirm ≥ 1×10^10 CFU/mL stock concentration
Genotype check: colony PCR for SpaCBA pilus locus on 5 random colonies

reagents.notes: Equivalent: DSM 33156. Do NOT use commercial probiotic capsules.

failure_warnings.mitigation: Single expansion → 100-aliquot working bank; never propagate beyond 5 passages from cryostock to dosing; document passage number on every gavage day

protocol.rationale: A single working stock with high-CFU verified aliquots locks strain identity for the whole study. SpaCBA PCR confirms pilus retention (the adhesion locus that drift selects against).

protocol.title: Calibrate FD-4 standard curve in pooled naïve mouse plasma
actions: Bleed 4 reserve mice (terminal cardiac under anaesthesia for max plasma)
Pool plasma; aliquot 50 µL × 30 wells
Spike with FD-4 stock to 8 standard concentrations (0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10 µg/mL)
Read on plate reader (485 nm ex / 520 nm em, top read, black 96-well plate)
Confirm linearity (R² > 0.99); compare slope to a buffer-only standard curve and document the matrix correction factor

failure_warnings.mitigation: Standard curve built in pooled mouse plasma (not buffer); reject any haemolysed pool samples; document slope difference from buffer curve
Wrap stock + plate in foil; read immediately after spiking

protocol.rationale: Plasma autofluorescence varies with vendor / age cohort. Pre-study calibration in this cohort's plasma fixes the matrix correction. A pre-built standard curve also de-risks the endpoint day.

protocol.title: Pilot — estimate baseline FD-4 SD (n = 4 mice)
actions: 4 acclimated reserve mice — 6 h fast, water ad lib
Gavage 600 mg/kg FD-4 in sterile PBS (200 µL via 22 G stainless ball-tip needle), foil-wrapped syringes
Submandibular bleed at 4 h post-gavage; plasma FD-4 fluorescence
Estimate within-group SD; recompute power; if n = 14/arm too low, escalate to 18/arm

failure_warnings.mitigation: If pilot mean FD-4 < 0.5 µg/mL OR SD/mean > 0.5, re-design: add a mild-DSS positive-control arm OR escalate primary endpoint to plasma LBP
Standardised 6-h daytime fast; remove food + visible crumbs; confirm cage cleanliness at gavage time

protocol.rationale: Power calculation from published data is unreliable because of [[wiki/failure-modes/FITC-dextran-dose-timing-heterogeneity]]. A 4-mouse pilot in the actual cohort gives the local SD before committing to the main n.

protocol.title: Daily LGG / PBS gavage × 28 days
actions: Each morning: thaw one LGG aliquot per arm, wash 1× in cold PBS, resuspend at 5×10^9 CFU/mL in cold sterile PBS
Keep on ice; gavage all LGG-arm mice within 90 min of prep (200 µL = 1×10^9 CFU/mouse via 22G needle)
PBS arm: same volume of sterile PBS
Plate dose-day CFU on MRS-agar for every cage from the gavage suspension; reject cohort day if < 80 % of design dose
Same time of day (08:00–10:00) every day; rotate animal-handler order arm-balanced
Weekly: collect 1 fresh faecal pellet per cage; CFU on MRS-agar (anaerobic) AND archive aliquot for end-of-study 16S sequencing
Daily welfare scoring: weight, posture, coat, activity. Adverse-event protocol triggered by > 10 % weight loss or unexpected illness.

failure_warnings.mitigation: Fresh prep daily; ice; 90-min cap; dose-day CFU on every cage; reject low-CFU days
Separate cages + alternate rack positions; dedicated PPE; weekly faecal LGG check on control cages — ANY positive = study integrity compromised
Daily welfare scoring; humane endpoint criteria pre-defined in IACUC; blood culture on any animal exceeding euthanasia thresholds
Endotoxin-grade water and consumables for LGG suspension prep; LAL-quantify endotoxin in one batch; report

protocol.rationale: Daily fresh prep with on-day CFU verification is the only way to rule out the silent viability-loss failure mode. Cage separation + weekly LGG-on-controls check is the only way to rule out coprophagy collapse. These two together are the experiment's load-bearing QC.

protocol.title: Endpoint FD-4 assay (Day 29)
actions: 06:00 — remove food + visible crumbs from all cages (start 6 h fast); water ad lib
12:00 — gavage 600 mg/kg FD-4 in sterile PBS (200 µL); foil-wrapped syringes; randomise gavage order across arms (block by cage)
16:00 (4 h post-gavage) — submandibular bleed under brief isoflurane; collect ≥ 75 µL plasma into EDTA tubes; keep on ice in foil
Plate-read same day on the pre-calibrated standard curve (re-run a 4-point standard alongside samples on the same plate)
Reject any haemolysed plasma samples; document

failure_warnings.mitigation: 6 h fast pre-registered, cage-cleanliness check at gavage time, log per-cage deviations
Foil-wrapped syringes + sample tubes throughout; read immediately
Re-run standard curve in pooled plasma on the same plate; reject visibly haemolysed samples
Pre-registered single dose × time pair (600 mg/kg, 4 h) — no other comparisons drawn from this assay

protocol.rationale: Single high-yield endpoint day, intensively QC'd, with all four FD-4 failure modes pre-mitigated.

protocol.title: Sacrifice + tissue harvest for mechanism overlay
actions: After endpoint bleed, terminal cardiac stick under deep anaesthesia; collect remaining plasma for LBP ELISA
Harvest distal ileum (2 cm), proximal colon (2 cm), distal colon (2 cm)
Bisect each segment: half into 10 % formalin for IF; half snap-frozen in liquid N₂ for Western blot + qPCR
Caecum content collected to pre-weighed cryotube; snap-freeze for SCFA GC analysis
Final faecal pellet collected for 16S sequencing (matched per mouse)

failure_warnings.mitigation: Adjacent-section design (formalin + frozen halves) lets us run paired WB + IF on every animal; if total vs membrane disagree, IF is closer to the relevant biology

protocol.rationale: Single sacrifice yields 5 readouts (FD-4 already done; + plasma LBP + tight-junction protein + caecal SCFA + 16S) — the orthogonal mechanism overlay.

protocol.title: Tight-junction Western blot + immunofluorescence (ileum)
protocol.duration_human: 3 days (1 day extraction, 1 day blot, 1 day IF)
actions: Mucosal scrape from frozen ileum; RIPA lysis; BCA quantification
WB: ZO-1 (Cell Signaling 13663), occludin (Invitrogen 71-1500), claudin-1 (Invitrogen 51-9000), claudin-2 (Invitrogen 32-5600); GAPDH + total-protein (Ponceau) normalisation
IF on adjacent formalin sections: ZO-1 + occludin co-stain with apical marker E-cadherin
Blinded quantification — analyst does not know treatment assignment until after scoring

reagents.qty: ZO-1 ab + occludin ab + claudin-1 ab + claudin-2 ab + secondaries
reagents.notes: Antibody catalog locked at study start — single lot for whole study

failure_warnings.mitigation: Single antibody lot; total-protein normalisation; blinded IF quantification; positive-control tissue (DSS-damaged colon) on every blot

protocol.rationale: WB + IF together provide the molecular substrate for any FD-4 phenotype. Blinding controls quantification bias.

protocol.title: Plasma LBP ELISA + caecal SCFA + 16S sequencing
actions: Plasma LBP: Hycult Biotech HK205 ELISA, all samples in duplicate on a single plate (n = 32 fits)
Caecal SCFA: GC-FID with 2-ethylbutyric acid internal standard; report µmol/g caecal content
Faecal 16S: V3–V4 amplicon on Illumina MiSeq, ≥ 30 000 reads/sample; LGG-specific welE qPCR on parallel aliquot for strain-level quantification
Cross-arm contamination check: if welE qPCR positive in any control-arm sample, investigate

failure_warnings.mitigation: 16S + welE qPCR is the post-hoc confirmation that the cage-design held — a positive control finding here invalidates the LGG-arm comparison

protocol.rationale: Plasma LBP gives orthogonal confirmation of barrier change. SCFA tests the SCFA-mediated mechanism. 16S confirms engraftment + cage integrity.

protocol.title: Statistical analysis + writeup
actions: Primary endpoint: two-sided Mann–Whitney U on plasma FD-4 (LGG vs PBS); 95% CI on percent reduction by bootstrap (10 000 resamples)
Secondary: Mann–Whitney on LBP, on tight-junction WB densitometry (ZO-1, occludin, claudin-1/2/-3/-4), on caecal SCFA
Engraftment confirmation: LGG-arm faecal LGG CFU > 0 throughout; control-arm = 0 throughout
Sensitivity analysis: exclude any cage with dose-day CFU < 80 % of design
Pre-registered: this is not a multi-comparison correction excuse — primary endpoint judged on its own α = 0.05; secondaries reported with FDR correction.
Pre-register on OSF before unblinding

failure_warnings.mitigation: Pre-registered analysis plan + commitment to publish regardless of direction (including null); cite the Singh 2014 retraction in the discussion as the precedent that didn't hold up
If pilot already escalated n or switched primary endpoint to LBP, document deviation transparently in the writeup

protocol.rationale: Pre-registration locks degrees of freedom. Single primary endpoint avoids fishing. Sensitivity analysis on dose-day CFU lets us salvage interpretability if some days were sub-spec.

materials.item: Mouse IL-10 + TNF-α + IL-6 multiplex (MSD U-Plex)
Western blot chemiluminescence + plates + tubes + tips + lysis buffer

budget.category: Consumables (WB, plates, tubes, lysis, gavage needles)

budget.notes: Direct costs only; assumes no equipment purchase

milestones: Pilot SD estimated → final n confirmed
Plasma + tissue archived for mechanism overlay

validation.primary_success_criterion: Mean plasma FD-4 in LGG arm ≤ 70% of PBS arm mean (≥ 30% reduction), p < 0.05 by two-sided Mann–Whitney U
validation.primary_failure_criterion: Plasma FD-4 LGG vs PBS difference < 30% OR not statistically significant at α = 0.05
secondary_corroborations: ZO-1 protein up in LGG-arm ileum (WB densitometry, p < 0.05)
Occludin protein up; claudin-1 up; claudin-2 down
Plasma LBP lower in LGG arm (consistent direction with FD-4)
Caecal butyrate higher in LGG arm
16S confirms LGG colonisation in treatment arm AND LGG absent from control arm
quality_gates: Dose-day CFU within 80–120% of design dose every day
FD-4 standard curve R² > 0.99 with matrix correction documented
No haemolysed plasma samples in primary analysis
16S faecal LGG present in 100% of LGG-arm samples and 0% of control-arm samples (any positive control = study integrity compromised)
All animals survive without humane-endpoint events
validation.reporting_commitment: Pre-registered on OSF before unblinding; results published regardless of direction (including null) within 6 months of analysis lock

failure_map.mitigation: Pilot estimate of SD; escalate n or switch primary endpoint to LBP if pilot signal suggests floor
Daily welfare; humane endpoints in IACUC; blood culture on any unexpected illness
Separate cages + alternate rack positions + dedicated PPE + weekly LGG-on-controls check + post-hoc 16S/welE confirmation
6 h pre-registered fast; cage-cleanliness check; per-cage deviation log
Fresh prep; ice; 90-min cap; dose-day CFU on every cage
Pre-registered single dose × time pair (600 mg/kg, 4 h); no cross-protocol pooling
Pre-registered analysis; commitment to publish null; explicit citation of Singh 2014 retraction
Make framing transparent in literature-QC verdict; pilot result triggers re-design decision; honest power discussion in writeup
Standard curve in pooled mouse plasma; reject haemolysed samples
Sealed master vial; ≤ 5 passages cryostock-to-dosing; SpaCBA PCR check
Endotoxin-grade water + consumables; LAL-quantify per batch
Single antibody lot; total-protein normalisation; blinded IF; positive-control tissue per blot

mechanism_diagram.title: What this experiment measures and why

edges.label: fewer leaks → less FD-4 in plasma
